pi_items.source: Task description and verifier.py path checks

privileged_info_summary: I have verified that the task uses real GPS sample data (fells_loop.gpx) corresponding to the Middlesex Fells Reservation from TopoGrafix. The required output waypoint name "Lunch_Stop", notes "Halfway rest point", and expected output path strictly match the prompt description and the verification logic. However, I have discovered a fatal defect in the task: the provided fells_loop.gpx file only contains <rtept> (route points), not <trkpt> (track points). Since the verifier independently recalculates the ground truth midpoint by directly parsing the un-modified C:\workspace\data\fells_loop.gpx file for <trkpt> tags, the `get_midpoint()` function will always return `None`, causing the verifier to perpetually fail with "Verifier Error: Failed to calculate true midpoint from track", rendering the task programmatically unscorable.
